scenario: A bank operates 2000 loan applications per day through a process that contains a joining parallel gateway with two incoming sequence flows. The team plans a migration that adds a third incoming sequence flow on the target gateway and needs to confirm what conditions the target gateway must satisfy for the migration of an in-flight joining gateway instance to be accepted.
question: Which condition must the target joining gateway satisfy for a joining parallel gateway migration to be accepted by the engine?

options.text: The target gateway must have at least the same number of incoming sequence flows as the source joining gateway so all previously taken flows can still be represented in the target
The target gateway must have exactly the same number of incoming sequence flows as the source gateway so the engine can pair every flow to a target flow on a one-to-one basis
The target gateway must have fewer incoming sequence flows than the source gateway so the engine can collapse pending sequence flows into a single waiting branch on migration
The target gateway must be converted into an inclusive gateway before migration so the engine can re-evaluate the conditions on the remaining incoming sequence flows after migration

a.text: Correct. For migrating a joining parallel or inclusive gateway, the target gateway must have at least the same number of incoming sequence flows as the source gateway. This guarantees that every previously taken sequence flow can be represented on the target.

b.text: Incorrect. The target gateway is allowed to have more incoming sequence flows than the source; the constraint is at least the same number, not exactly the same. Process changes that add new incoming flows are explicitly supported.

c.text: Incorrect. Fewer incoming sequence flows would lose information about flows that have already been taken on the source gateway. The migration rule requires at least as many incoming sequence flows on the target, not fewer.

d.text: Incorrect. There is no requirement to convert a joining parallel gateway into an inclusive gateway before migration. Mapping instructions cannot change the element type, so the gateway must remain the same type across source and target.

explanation: When migrating a joining parallel or inclusive gateway, the target gateway must have at least the same number of incoming sequence flows as the source. The gateway and the relevant taken sequence flow must also be referenced by mapping instructions in the migration plan.
